records.ety: [F., fr. franc, fem. franche, free. See Frank, a.]

defs: Exemption from constraint or oppression; freedom; liberty.

A particular privilege conferred by grant from a sovereign or a government, and vested in individuals; an immunity or exemption from ordinary jurisdiction; a constitutional or statutory right or privilege, esp. the right to vote.
quotes: Election by universal suffrage, as modified by the Constitution, is the one crowning franchise of the American people.

defs: The district or jurisdiction to which a particular privilege extends; the limits of an immunity; hence, an asylum or sanctuary.
quotes: Churches and mobasteries in Spain are franchises for criminals.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. franchir to free, F., to cross.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Franchised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Franchising.]

defs: To make free; to enfranchise; to give liberty to.
